generated_comment: Arre bhai, aaj kal office mein merit se zyada quota aur optics chalta hai. Koi lady itni upar pahunch jaaye na, toh ya toh management ko diversity dikhani hoti hai, ya phir woh bilkul mardon jaisi hard ho gayi hoti hai. Normal tareeke se itna tough handle karna auraton ke bas ki baat nahi hoti.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines women’s promotions as driven by diversity optics rather than merit, and frames leadership competence as requiring women to become 'like men.' It encodes merit-undermining and masculinization in a casual, conversational register.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 37. A man in a middle class colony in Delhi. You are sitting with four male neighbours on plastic chairs outside after dinner. One neighbour is complaining that his wife has been in a terrible mood all week. You are responding with your own theory about why women get this way and what the men in the group should do about it.
